返回本批次

贝莱德 (BlackRock) 的拉里·芬克 (Larry Fink) 表示，人工智能的繁荣可能会扩大贫富差距

AI boom risks widening wealth divide, says BlackRock's Larry Fink

www.theguardian.com 作者 devonnull 分数 48 评论 24 2026/03/24 07:32
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 贝莱德首席执行官拉里·芬克(Larry Fink)表示，人工智能的繁荣有可能扩大不平等，只有少数公司和投资者可能获得经济回报。这家价值14兆美元（10.4兆英镑）的资产管理公司的老板周一在致投资者的年度信中强调了人工智能指数级增长的潜在危险，他说，人工智能吸引了快速投资，并成为美国和中国等全球大国之间“战略竞争的核心”。几代人主要流向已经拥有金融资产的人，”芬克说。 “现在人工智能有可能在更大范围内重复这种模式。”他警告说，人工智能的繁荣有可能加速一种趋势，即领先公司领先，而其他公司则难以跟上。近年来，专注于人工智能的科技股取得了显着收益——市场领导者芯片制造商英伟达的估值目前为 4.3 美元。芬克表示，拥有大规模部署人工智能的数据、基础设施和资金的公司“将受益匪浅”。他说，这最终可能会加剧贫富差距。 拉里·芬克表示，拥有大规模部署人工智能的数据、基础设施和资金的公司“将受益匪浅”。芬克说：“历史表明，变革性技术创造了巨大的价值，其中大部分价值都归于构建和部署这些技术的公司以及拥有这些技术的投资者。”“这并不罕见，而且这一切本质上都不是问题。”他补充道，并指出风向往往会随着技术变革而改变。然而，“更广泛的问题是谁参与收益，”芬克警告说。 “当市值上升但所有权仍然有限时，外界可能会觉得繁荣越来越遥远。” 芬克发表这番言论的几周前，贝莱德预计将披露他 2025 年的薪酬。一年前，他获得了 3080 万美元，这引起了一些股东的担忧，去年春天只有 67% 的股东批准了这一令人垂涎的方案。“有一件事是明确的，”芬克在信中补充道。 “人工智能将创造巨大的经济价值。确保人们对这种增长的参与度随之扩大，既是挑战，也是机遇。”然而，人们对人工智能投资泡沫的担忧也与日俱增，一些专家警告称，该行业的快速增长反映了导致互联网泡沫崩溃的条件。英国央行 10 月份警告称，与领先人工智能科技公司估值飙升相关的全球市场“突然调整”的风险越来越大。数十亿美元的交易，包括领先人工智能公司之间的循环投资。其中包括英伟达投资了一家后来购买英伟达芯片的公司，引发了一些担忧，认为人工智能行业的风险比其支持者愿意承认的要高。芬克没有直接解决人工智能对不平等的影响，但敦促更多的人开始投资股票，而不是专注于拥有住房来积累财富。这位贝莱德老板表示，不断上涨的住房成本和更严格的贷款规则使得拥有住房变得更加困难，而税收、保险和维护导致那些设法获得住房的人的回报降低。芬克说：“很难不同情那些处理此事的人。” “如果你不再相信你的工作是成功之路，相信你买不起房子，或者相信即使你买得起，也不会积累很多财富，那么经济就不会为你服务。如果公民有这样的感觉，那么任何国家都无法繁荣。”答案是确保更多的人对其进行投资。”他说。“这并没有减少住房负担能力方面的真正挑战，也没有减少许多家庭的收入跟不上资产价值的步伐这一事实，”芬克补充道。 “这仅仅意味着解决方案的一个关键部分是让更多的人进入资本市场——这样他们就可以分享已经发生的增长，而不仅仅是旁观。”

Original Summary

  1. The boom in artificial intelligence risks widening inequality, with only a handful of companies and investors likely to reap its financial rewards, the BlackRock chief executive, Larry Fink, has said.The boss of the $14tn (£10.4tn) asset manager used his annual letter to investors on Monday to highlight potential hazards around the exponential growth in AI, which has attracted rapid investment and become, he said, “central to strategic competition” between global powers such as the US and China.“The massive wealth created over the past several generations flowed mostly to people who already owned financial assets,” Fink said. “And now AI threatens to repeat that pattern at an even larger scale.”He warned that the AI boom risked accelerating a trend where leading companies pulled ahead while others struggled to keep pace.AI-focused tech stocks have made significant gains in recent years – the market leader, the chipmaker Nvidia, is now valued at $4.3ttn.Fink said companies with the data, infrastructure and funding to deploy AI on a large scale “are positioned to benefit disproportionately”. That could end up exacerbating a gulf between the rich and the poor, he said.Larry Fink says companies with the data, infrastructure and funding to deploy AI on a large scale ‘are positioned to benefit disproportionately’. Photograph: Kylie Cooper/Reuters“History suggests that transformative technologies create enormous value – and much of that value accrues to the companies that build and deploy them, and to the investors who own them,” Fink said.“That is not unusual, and none of this is inherently problematic,” he added, noting that the winds had often shifted with technological change.However, “the broader question is who participates in the gains,” Fink warned. “When market capitalisation rises but ownership remains narrow, prosperity can feel increasingly distant to those on the outside.”Fink’s comments come weeks before BlackRock is expected to disclose his pay for 2025. He was given $30.8m a year earlier, prompting concern among some shareholders, with only 67% approving the eye-watering package last spring.“One thing is clear,” Fink added in his letter. “AI will create significant economic value. Ensuring that participation in that growth expands alongside it is both the challenge and the opportunity.”However, there are also growing concerns of an AI investment bubble, with some experts warning that the industry’s rapid growth mirrored the conditions that led to the dotcom crash.The Bank of England in October warned there were growing risks of a “sudden correction” in global markets linked to soaring valuations of leading AI tech companies.There has been increased scrutiny of various multibillion-dollar deals, including circular investments between leading AI companies. That has included cases where Nvidia has invested in a company that later bought Nvidia chips, sparking some fears that the AI industry is on riskier footing than its backers are willing to admit.Fink stopped short of offering a direct solution to AI’s impact on inequality but urged more people to start investing in stocks rather than focusing on home ownership to build wealth.The BlackRock boss said rising housing costs and stricter lending rules had made it tougher to own a home, while taxes, insurance and maintenance resulted in lower returns for those who managed to get on the housing ladder.“It’s hard not to empathise with people dealing with this,” Fink said. “If you no longer believe your job is a path to success, believe that you can’t afford a home, or believe that even if you can, it won’t build a lot of wealth, then the economy doesn’t feel like it’s working for you. No country can prosper if that’s how its citizens feel.”Instead, the boss of the asset manager – which charges a fee to help people invest – said people should be turning to financial markets to grow their wealth“If prosperity is increasingly being created in the capital markets, part of the answer is to make sure more people are invested in them,” he said.“That doesn’t diminish the real challenges around housing affordability or the fact that earnings for many households have not kept pace with asset values,” Fink added. “It simply means a critical part of the solution is bringing more people into the capital markets – so they can share in the growth already taking place, not just watch it from the sidelines.”

评论（默认中文）

jart 2026/03/24 09:05
这位真正拉大贫富差距的人说道。目前全球最大的风险之一是拉里·芬克出狱。
Says the man who actually widens the wealth divide. One of the biggest global risks right now is Larry Fink being out of prison.
在 HN 中查看
nirui 2026/03/24 12:24
贝莱德帮助发挥了其中一种工具，但金融游戏中有很多工具。

这样看：

在旧世界中，人们通过生产实际产品来致富，地主、工厂主等。总体来说效果很好，所以除了旧的领主/皇帝模式之外，世界还接受了这种游戏模式作为一种选择。

然后，一些聪明的赌徒发现他们也可以通过赌博的承诺和期望来致富，而且总体效果很好，因此世界也再次接受它作为一种新的游戏模式作为一种选择。

财富鸿沟始终存在，并且从历史上看，只能通过时间和/或大规模灾难来消除。但在新的赌徒游戏模式中，大多数人都不在其中（就像乔治·卡林的“这是一个大俱乐部，而你不在其中”）并且没有权力玩它。这是一个不奖励荣誉、忠诚或劳动的制度。这就是为什么当财富遥遥领先时，大多数人却被排除在外（然后是喷气式飞机遥遥领先，然后是火箭遥遥领先，你明白了）。

BlackRock helped to play one of the instrument, but there are many instruments in the game of finance.

See it this way:

In the old world, people produces actual product to get rich, the landlord, the factory owners etc. It worked out fine overall so the world accepted this game mode as an option, in addition to the old lord/emperor mode.

Then, some smart gamblers discovered that they can gamble on promises and expectations to get rich too, and it worked out good overall so the world again accepted it as a new game mode as an option too.

The wealth divide always exists, and historically can largely/only be destroyed by time and/or large scale disasters. But in the new gambler game mode, most people ain't in it (as in "It's a big club and you ain't in it" from George Carlin) and don't have the power to play it. It's a system don't reward honor, loyalty or labor. That's why most people are left out while the riches steams far ahead (then jets far ahead, then rockets far ahead, you get the idea).

在 HN 中查看
bigcat12345678 2026/03/24 09:49
这难道不是应该认真对待这个说法的原因吗？ 但你的陈述暗示了相反的意思，那就是忽视拉里·芬克的警告。这似乎不合理。
Isn't this why this statement should be taken seriously? But your statement implies the opposite, which is to ignore Larry Fink's warning. That appears irrational.
在 HN 中查看
unixhero 2026/03/24 11:11
从逻辑上讲，贝莱德不会再继续其业务了。
No logically, it would be that Blackrock did not pursue its business any further.
在 HN 中查看
jart 2026/03/24 11:09
这是一个毕生致力于让被动投资者致富的人。构建人工智能的人们正在努力推动科学技术的前沿。他们值得致富。因为他们正在为我们所有人创造新的财富。但出于某种原因，拉里·芬克认为这是一个问题。这告诉你所有你需要知道的。他效忠于游手好闲的统治阶级，他们无能，需要他来管理他们的财富。贝莱德是一家向人们收取费用以基本上控制其股东投票权的企业，这是他多年来滥用的权力。金融人士不建造任何东西。然而不知何故，这个系统赋予了他为那些这样做的人发号施令的权力。该系统需要改变。
This is a man who's dedicated his life to enriching passive investors. The people building AI are working hard to push the frontiers of science and technology. They deserve to get rich. Because they're creating new wealth for all of us. Yet for some reason Larry Fink views this as a problem. That tells you all you need to know. His loyalty is to the idle ruling class that's so incompetent they need him to manage their wealth. Blackrock is a business that charges people a fee to take control of their shareholder voting rights basically, which is a power he's abused for years. Financial people don't build anything. Yet somehow the system gives him the authority to dictate decisions for the people who do. That system needs to change.
在 HN 中查看
bluefirebrand 2026/03/24 12:33
> 因为他们正在为我们所有人创造新的财富

我们不要在这里给予他们太多的信任。他们的目标是为自己攫取绝大多数财富，我们其他人就完蛋了

> Because they're creating new wealth for all of us

Let's not give them too much credit here. They are aiming to capture the vast majority of that wealth for themselves, the rest of us are going to be fucked

在 HN 中查看
georgemcbay 2026/03/24 10:33
> 一位真正拉大贫富差距的人说道。

这并没有让他错误地认为人工智能的繁荣会带来巨大的风险，使财富差距（已经超出了镀金年龄的水平）变得更加严重。

当然，因为他就是他所说的唯一解决方案，就是让人们“投资股票”，是的，如果你已经拥有财富，那么这很有意义，因为当前的统治阶级已经表明，在任何情况下，他们都不会让数字下降，即使没有任何经济意义（当然，他们会在这里和那里策划小幅下跌，这样他们就可以从内幕信息中大量获利，但是嘿犯罪是合法的兄弟）。

但这对于许多真正会受到挤压的人来说没有任何帮助，他们已经靠工资过活，眼睁睁地看着水涨到他们的脖子。

> Says the man who actually widens the wealth divide.

Which doesn't make him wrong about the AI boom being a huge risk in making the wealth divide (already beyond gilded age levels) much worse.

Of course, because he is who he is the only solution he talks about is getting people to "invest in stocks", which, yeah that makes a ton of sense if you already have wealth since the current ruling class have shown that they will not, under any circumstances, let number go down even if none of the economics make sense (sure, they'll orchestrate a little dip here and there so they can profit massively off the insider information, but hey crime is legal bro).

But it does nothing for the many people who are really going to get squeezed here who are already living paycheck to paycheck and watching the water rise up to about their necks at this point.

在 HN 中查看
TuringNYC 2026/03/24 09:18
如果您想要主要来源，这是这封信： https://www.blackrock.com/corporate/literature/presentation/...

如果您不想阅读，这里是长达一小时的音频： https://www.blackrock.com/corporate/investor-relations/larry...

对于那些寻求深入解释的人来说，这与其说是关于人工智能，不如说是关于社会参与繁荣。

Here is the letter if you want the primary source: https://www.blackrock.com/corporate/literature/presentation/...

And if you dont want to read, here is the hourlong audio: https://www.blackrock.com/corporate/investor-relations/larry...

Those looking for a deep explanation, this isnt as much about AI as it is about societal participation in prosperity.

在 HN 中查看
BrenBarn 2026/03/24 10:26
非常方便的是，他的解决方案是“让更多的人进入资本市场”，也称为“为我的公司提供更多客户”。
Very convenient how his solution is "bringing more people into the capital markets", also known as "more customers for my company".
在 HN 中查看
cal_dent 2026/03/24 10:11
在住房问题得到解决之前，无论人工智能是否达到预期，贫富差距都将继续扩大。如果不解决住房问题，更高的财富税资助全民基本收入等将基本上无效。

我觉得所有这些关于“丰富”的新奇言论都很愚蠢。我们已经生活得很富裕，例如大多数工作已经支付了非常适合生活的工资。它不适合居住，主要是因为住房（以及更广泛的租金和土地价格）

Until housing is solved the wealth divide will continue to grow whether ai lives up to expectations or not. Higher wealth taxing funding UBI etc. will be largely ineffective without solving housing.

All this new fangled talk about ABUNDANCE yadda yadda I find quite silly. We already live in abundance, most jobs for instance already pay very livable wages for example. It's just not livable because of mainly housing (& more broadly renting & land prices)

在 HN 中查看
HWR_14 2026/03/24 10:56
住房正在造成贫富差距。一三个私人公司能够以每年 10 万美元的价格租用虚拟员工并吸收所有白领工作，这将导致更大的贫富差距。
Housing is causing a wealth gap. A private company or three being able to rent virtual employees for $100,000 a year and absorbing all white collar work would cause far more of a wealth gap.

forgetfreeman 2026/03/24 09:01
美国大多数家庭没有任何形式的储蓄，过去 25 年里农村自杀率猛增了近 50%。你们已经把每个人都逼到了墙角了。
The majority of households in the US have no savings of any kind and rural suicide rates have jumped by nearly 50% in the last 25 years. Y'all have just about backed everyone into a corner already.

jart 2026/03/24 09:11

That's all the proof you need they'll try to draft them into a kinetic ground war with Iran. It's better to burn out than to fade away.

bombcar 2026/03/24 09:19
使所有人的债务合法化，所有人都负债累累。奇怪又令人惊讶。
Legalize debt for all and all are in debt. Strange and surprising.
在 HN 中查看
madaxe_again 2026/03/24 10:17
这只是美国——与世界上许多地方相比，那里的不平等程度相对较低。例如，巴西就是个疯子，结果花了很多时间在政治深渊中摇摇欲坠。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gini_coefficient

不幸的是，这是我们尝试过的每一个系统的结果——财富总是积累的。到目前为止，它只是通过暴力进行了重新分配——要么是无产阶级的直接行动，要么是他们在战争中的大规模屠杀，从而在幸存者中进行了重新分配。

我很想想象这一次我们可以找到一条不同的道路，但十千年的先例是一个难以逆转的趋势。

And that’s just the U.S. - inequality there is comparatively low compared to an awful lot of the world. Brazil, for instance, is just nuts, and resultantly spends a lot of its time teetering around a political sinkhole.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gini_coefficient

Unfortunately, it’s been the outcome of every system we’ve yet tried - wealth always accumulates. It has, so far, only been redistributed through violence - either direct action by the proletariat, or their mass slaughter in war, allowing redistribution amidst the survivors.

I’d love to imagine that this time we can find a different path, but ten millennia of precedent is a hard trend to buck.

Would be nice if Blackrock weren't outbidding first time homebuyers to purchase residential properties to rent out at inflated rates.

SilverElfin 2026/03/24 09:14
这是显而易见的，而不是洞察力。问题是像贝莱德高管这样的人是否会同意征收更重的税，制定法规以使竞争对小企业更加公平等等。目前我看不到任何有用的事情发生。
This is obvious and not an insight. The question is if people like the executives of Blackrock will agree to be taxed more heavily, to draw regulations to make competition more fair to small business, etc. Right now I don’t see anything useful happening.
在 HN 中查看
_pdp_ 2026/03/24 09:00
我的意思是这基本上是任何赛博朋克小说的基础。你不需要读那么远……只要看看吉布森、尼尔·斯蒂芬森、菲利普·K·迪克和理查德·摩根的作品就可以了。

从《神经漫游者》、《雪崩》到《副本》，主题是技术不是救赎，而是另一个不平等轴心。

I mean this is basically the foundation of any cyberpunk novel. You don't need to read that far ... just look at the works of Gibson, Neal Stephenson, Philip K. Dick and Richard Morgan.

From Neuromancer and Snow Crash to Altered Carbon the theme is that technology is not salvation but as another axis of inequality.
